[[User:Firefangledfeathers|Firefangledfeathers]] ([[User talk:Firefangledfeathers|talk]] / [[Special:Contributions/Firefangledfeathers|contribs]]) 16:07, 17 May 2025 (UTC) {{tps}} Editors should keep in mind that there is no policy on Wikipedia that gives any editor the power to impose a moratorium, which just says to editors that another move request should not be started again for a specified period of time. We are only able to suggest to other editors that they "self-impose" a moratorium to not begin a fresh move request for a specified period of time. That's how I usually say it, so that the editors who are unfamiliar with the term, "moratorium", are able to understand it along with everyone else. Just sayin', and thank you both for your eyes on Wikipedia''!'' '''''[[User:Paine Ellsworth|P.I. Ellsworth]]''''' , [[Editor|ed.]] [[User talk:Paine Ellsworth|put'er there]] 18:23, 17 May 2025 (UTC) :This may require some discussion and possibly a policy change. While I believe you are correct about written policy, in practice, it seems to me that there is broad support for summarily closing or removing discussions if they violate a moratorium previously imposed by a closer. —[[User:Compassionate727|Compassionate727]] ([[User talk:Compassionate727|T]]·[[Special:Contributions/Compassionate727|C]]) 12:49, 18 May 2025 (UTC) ::I've taken the liberty of creating a page notice at [[Template:Editnotices/Page/Talk:Turkey]] advising editors of the moratorium, and the consensus for removing or terminating any such discussion, that will be displayed to anyone creating a new discussion between now and 16 May 2026. ::I believe that the guidelines about disruptive editing cover the ability to impose moratoriums. Endless creations of new discussions of the same matters are disruptive to editors with the page on their watchlists likely to become weary of the incessant pinging about the same matter that's already been disposed of only a short time ago. Such repeated discussions also drown out discussions of other matters because editors watching the page become likely to tune it out as they peruse their watchlists and not notice that something else is being discussed for a change. So it's unfair to editors who have something else to bring up for discussion. (I understand that this isn't the same as classic disruptive editing that's intended to be disruptive. But it's disruptive nonetheless.) [[User:Largoplazo|Largoplazo]] ([[User talk:Largoplazo|talk]]) 13:38, 18 May 2025 (UTC) ::I know of no closer who has the power to impose a moratorium. Even your words to supplement this closure recognize that it was a consensus of editors that imposed the moratorium. In the past there have been editors who pushed back even at that. Since policy is lean on the subject, a closer should always be prepared (as usual) to cordially and thoroughly explain a decision when asked to do so. '''''[[User:Paine Ellsworth|P.I. Ellsworth]]''''' , [[Editor|ed.]] [[User talk:Paine Ellsworth|put'er there]] 17:28, 18 May 2025 (UTC) :::I'm not quite sure what point you're making. :::* Are you merely taking issue with the technical correctness of the phrase {{tq|a moratorium previously imposed by a closer}}, in place of something like "a moratorium that a closer has found to have been reached"? :::* Are you taking issue with the notion that a closer, in giving their summation of whether there's a consensus for deletion or a page move or whatever and what that consensus is, should do the same regarding any calls for a moratorium that came up in the course of the discussion? :::* Are you taking issue with the notion that a moratorium is now enforceable? :::[[User:Largoplazo|Largoplazo]] ([[User talk:Largoplazo|talk]]) 19:09, 18 May 2025 (UTC) ::::I would say probably the last issue. And only because I suppose I missed the policy that enforces such moratoriums. We have policies that enforce user blocks and policies that enforce other actions taken against vandalism and other types of disruptive editing. Enforcing moratoriums has always been a gray area. I'm just doing a little devil's advocating, so please don't think I'm not in agreement. I've always wanted to see more policy strength when it comes to moratoriums. Up to now, if an editor wants to challenge a moratorium, there really isn't much the rest of us can do about it that's backed by p&g. '''''[[User:Paine Ellsworth|P.I. Ellsworth]]''''' , [[Editor|ed.]] [[User talk:Paine Ellsworth|put'er there]] 19:24, 18 May 2025 (UTC) :::::We can report disruptive editing, editing contrary to consensus. Not upon initial posting, of course, there can't be any presumption that the user knew about the moratorium. But repeated posts following a direct explanation amount to disruptive editing. [[User:Largoplazo|Largoplazo]] ([[User talk:Largoplazo|talk]]) 19:28, 18 May 2025 (UTC) ::::::Yes, of course – you and I have probably both seen it go more than one way depending on how strong an argument a moratorium-opposing editor can make and if other editors perceive it as disruptive or not. When it comes to imposing and enforcing a moratorium, that's a specific issue that remains to be fully addressed. '''''[[User:Paine Ellsworth|P.I. Ellsworth]]''''' , [[Editor|ed.]] [[User talk:Paine Ellsworth|put'er there]] 19:43, 18 May 2025 (UTC) :::::::[[WP:MORATORIUM]] exists. [[User:Largoplazo|Largoplazo]] ([[User talk:Largoplazo|talk]]) 15:42, 19 May 2025 (UTC) ::::::::And it's all we've got, isn't it. MORATORIUM's been in existence for more than 10 years, and it's still just an essay – unvetted by the community. Those editors who try too hard to restrain a moratorium-challenging editor should be wary of this pitfall/abyss. Madhavan**. - It is produced and directed by **Aditya Dhar**, who is already notable for ''Uri: The Surgical Strike''. - The article is backed by multiple **reliable, independent sources** (e.g., Times of India, Economic Times) covering the production, casting, and announcement of the film in depth. - Meets [[WP:NFF]] point 1: The film has been the subject of **significant coverage** in multiple reliable sources that are independent of the subject. --- '''2. [[Murderbaad]]''' - This is an upcoming Hindi romantic-thriller backed by **Reliance Entertainment** and directed by **Arnab Chatterjee**. - It features well-known actors like **Sharib Hashmi** and **Amole Gupte**. - Covered in several notable sources like **Filmfare**, **Bollywood Hungama**, and **News18**, including detailed discussions on plot, cast, trailer release, and music. - Thus, it also meets the [[WP:NFF]] guideline as a future film with '''significant media coverage'''. --- While both articles did meet notability criteria, I’ve added maintenance tags: * `{{tx|Bare URLs}}` – because many citations were unformatted or raw links. * `{{tx|Lead too short}}` – because the lead section in both articles needs to summarize key elements of the article. [[User:GoldRomean|GoldRomean]] ([[User talk:GoldRomean|talk]]) 16:02, 17 July 2025 (UTC) == Question from [[User:Dojo meister|Dojo meister]] (19:00, 18 July 2025) == Hello i need to make a post about a public figure but i wanted to find out about the dos and donts. It seems my account is new so i risk being banned for that? --[[User:Dojo meister|Dojo meister]] ([[User talk:Dojo meister|talk]]) 19:00, 18 July 2025 (UTC) :{{Ping|Dojo Meister}} Hello there, You need to be carefully creating articles about public figures, especially from a new account, can raise red flags if not done carefully. Here are a few dos and don'ts to keep in mind. * Make sure the person is notable by [[H:FIRST|Wikipedia standards]] (significant coverage in [[WP:RS|reliable, independent sources]] like [[newspapers]], [[books]], etc.).Use neutral language—avoid praise, hype, or [[WP:SPAM|promotion]] and Provide [[WP:V|verifiable]] references with proper citations and Start in [[Special:MyPage/sandbox|your sandbox]] or as a [[WP:DRAFT|draf]], not directly in the [[WP:WIAA|main article space]]. * Don’t copy-paste from other sites that’s a [[WP:CV|copyright violation]] and don’t write about friends, yourself, or clients, it leads to conflict of interest issues and don’t rely on social media or personal blogs as sources. * Don’t be discouraged if the draft is rejected; you can always improve it with better sources and Creating a solid draft first and asking for feedback at places Visit the [[Wikipedia:Teahouse|Teahouse]] or [[WP:AFC|Articles for Creation]] is a safe route.Let me know if you need help checking notability or sources! --Warm Regards,[[User:Abhiimanyu7| Abhimanyu7]] [[User talk:Abhiimanyu7| talk ]] 03:54, 19 July 2025 (UTC) == Hello == Please help review my article: [[Draft:Leader of the Ruling Party in the National Assembly (Nepal)]], [[Draft:Vice-Chairperson of the National Assembly (Nepal)]] [[Special:Contributions/2001:DF7:BE80:1ED:5C4:CA16:AEE3:B446|2001:DF7:BE80:1ED:5C4:CA16:AEE3:B446]] ([[User talk:2001:DF7:BE80:1ED:5C4:CA16:AEE3:B446|talk]]) 10:40, 20 July 2025 (UTC) @[[Special:Contributions/2001:DF7:BE80:1ED:5C4:CA16:AEE3:B446|2001:DF7:BE80:1ED:5C4:CA16:AEE3:B446]] Hi there, Thank you for your draft submission. However, I am declining this draft at this time due to the following concerns: '''1st Draft Review Result''' - '''Notable Status = Failed''' *No significant independent reliable sources focus solely on the Vice-Chairperson role. * Already Covered Elsewhere – Information about the position is typically included under broader articles like National Assembly (Nepal) or Chairperson. * Notability Not Established – The role lacks standalone encyclopedic notability per Wikipedia guidelines (no in-depth analysis or coverage). '''2nd Draft Review Result''' - '''Notable Status = Failed''' * The Vice-Chairperson of the National Assembly (Nepal) is a constitutional role, but few reasons are mentioned below -: * It lacks significant independent coverage in reliable sources. * Most mentions are in passing within broader articles about the National Assembly or Chairperson.* No clear, independent notability established for the office itself or for past officeholders outside other roles. [[User:DentistRecommended|DentistRecommended]] ([[User talk:DentistRecommended|talk]]) 13:42, 31 July 2025 (UTC) == Question from [[User:McDaniel666|McDaniel666]] on [[Draft:Kardi keyzsa]] (11:24, 1 August 2025) == Hello how can I publish my draft to the internet --[[User:McDaniel666|McDaniel666]] ([[User talk:McDaniel666|talk]]) 11:24, 1 August 2025 (UTC) :Hi {{Ping|McDaniel666}}, thanks for your question. If you'd like to publish your draft to the wider public on Wikipedia, you'll need to go through the [[WP:AFC|Articles for Creation]] (AfC) review process. Since your page is currently in Draft space, look near the top of the page for a blue button that says "Submit your draft for review." When you click that, your draft will be sent to a volunteer reviewer who will look it over and check that it meets Wikipedia's basic requirements for inclusion, including notability, reliable sourcing, and neutrality and Once a reviewer approves the draft, it will be moved out of the Draft namespace and into main article space, making it a live Wikipedia article. Keep in mind that this can sometimes take a few days depending on the review queue, but you will be notified when a decision is made or Before submitting, double-check that your sources are from independent and reputable publications and that they clearly establish why your subject is notable. Avoid promotional or autobiographical language and stick to a neutral tones and If your draft is declined, do not worry. The reviewer will usually leave comments explaining what needs improvement. You can make changes and resubmit at any time. Let me know if you'd like help reviewing your draft before submission or if you have any questions about the process. You're doing great by taking the time to ask and learn. Good luck and welcome to Wikipedia editing.--Warm Regards,[[User:Abhiimanyu7| Abhimanyu7]] [[User talk:Abhiimanyu7| talk ]] 12:55, 1 August 2025 (UTC) == Temporary account IP checker granted == [[File:TAIV Wikipedia.svg|right|80px|alt=The temporary account IP viewer logo, composed of the Wikipedia globe with a user and an IP address]] Hello, Abhiimanyu7. [[File:Green check.svg|20px|alt=]] '''Guideline and policy news''' * Following [[Wikipedia talk:Speedy deletion#RFC: New CSD for unreviewed LLM content|a request for comment]], a new speedy deletion criterion, [[WP:G15|G15]], has been enacted. It applies to pages generated by a [[large language model]] (LLM) without human review. * Following [[Wikipedia:Requests for comment/Temporary account IP-viewer|a request for comment]], there is [[WP:TAIV|a new policy]] outlining the granting of permissions to view the IP addresses of [[mw:Trust and Safety Product/Temporary Accounts|temporary accounts]]. Temporary account deployment on the English Wikipedia is currently scheduled for September 2025, and editors can [[WP:PERM/TAIV|request access to the permission]] ahead of time. Admins are encouraged to keep an eye on the request page; there will likely be a flood of editors requesting the permission when they realize they can no longer see IP addresses. [[File:Octicons-tools.svg|20px|alt=]] '''Technical news''' * Administrators can now restrict the "Add a Link" feature to newcomers. The [[mw:Special:MyLanguage/Help:Growth/Tools/Add a link|"Add a Link"]] Structured Task [[mw:Special:MyLanguage/Growth/Constructive activation experimentation#Enwiki A/B test & "Add a Link" Improvements (Wiki Experiences 1.2.11 & 1.2.16)|helps new account holders get started with editing]]. Administrators can configure this setting in the [[Special:CommunityConfiguration/GrowthSuggestedEdits|Community Configuration]] page. [[File:Scale of justice 2.svg|20px|alt=]] '''Arbitration''' * The arbitration case ''[[Special:Permalink/1301421643#Wikipedia:Arbitration/Requests/Case/Indian military history closed|Indian military history]]'' has been closed. ** South Asia ([[WP:CT/SA]]) is designated a [[WP:Contentious topics|contentious topic]]. The topic area is specifically defined as {{tq|All pages related to the region of [[South Asia]] (India, Pakistan, Afghanistan, Sri Lanka, Bangladesh and Nepal), broadly construed, including but not limited to history, politics, ethnicity, and social groups.}} ** The contentious topic designations for [[WP:CT/SL|Sri Lanka]] (SL) and [[WP:CT/IPA|India, Pakistan, and Afghanistan]] (IPA) are folded into this new contentious topic. ** The community-authorized general sanctions regarding [[Wikipedia:General sanctions/South Asian social groups|South Asian social groups]] (GS/CASTE) are rescinded and folded into this new contentious topic. * The arbitration case ''[[Wikipedia:Arbitration/Requests/Case/Article titles and capitalisation 2|Article titles and capitalisation 2]]'' has been opened. Evidence submissions in this case closed on 31 July. * The arbitration case ''[[Wikipedia:Arbitration/Requests/Case/Transgender healthcare and people|Transgender healthcare and people]]'' has been opened. Evidence submissions in this case will close on 11 August. [[File:Info Simple bw.svg|20px|alt=]] '''Miscellaneous''' * [[:wikimania:2025:Wikimania|Wikimania 2025]] is happening in [[Nairobi]], [[Kenya]], and online from August 6 to August 9. This year marks 20 years of [[Wikimania]]. Interested users can join the online event. Registration for the virtual event is free and will remain open throughout Wikimania. This message is being sent to remind you of significant upcoming changes regarding logged-out editing. '''Starting 4 November, logged-out editors will no longer have their IP address publicly displayed.''' Instead, they will have a '''[[:mw:Trust and Safety Product/Temporary Accounts|temporary account]]''' (TA) associated with their edits. Users with some extended rights like administrators and CheckUsers, as well as users with the [[WP:TAIV|temporary account IP viewer]] (TAIV) user right will still be able to reveal temporary users' IP addresses and all contributions made by temporary accounts from a specific IP address or range. '''How do temporary accounts work?''' [[File:Editing with Temporary Accounts.webm|thumb|350px|Editing from a temporary account]] * When a logged-out user completes an edit or a logged action for the first time, a cookie will be set in this user's browser and a temporary account tied with this cookie will be automatically created for them. This account's name will follow the pattern: ~2025-12345-67 (a tilde, year of creation, a number split into units of 5). * All subsequent actions by the temporary account user will be attributed to this username. The cookie will expire 90 days after its creation. As long as it exists, all edits made from this device will be attributed to this temporary account. It will be the same account even if the IP address changes, unless the user clears their cookies or uses a different device or web browser. * A record of the IP address used at the time of each edit will be stored for 90 days after the edit. Users with the [[WP:TAIV|temporary account IP viewer]] (TAIV) user right will be able to see the underlying IP addresses. * As a measure against vandalism, there are two limitations on the creation of temporary accounts: ** There has to be a minimum of 10 minutes between subsequent temporary account creations from the same IP (or /64 range in case of IPv6). ** There can be a maximum of 6 temporary accounts created from an IP (or /64 range) within a period of 24 hours. '''Temporary account IP viewer user right''' [[File:Temporary Accounts demo - IP Reveal.webm|thumb|350px|How to enable IP Reveal]] * Administrators may grant the '''[[WP:TAIV|temporary account IP viewer]]''' (TAIV) user right to non-administrators who meet the [[WP:TAIVGRANT|criteria for granting]]. Importantly, an editor must make an explicit request for the permission (e.g. at [[WP:PERM/TAIV]])—administrators are not permitted to assign the right without a request. * Administrators will automatically be able to see temporary account IP information once they have accepted the [[wmf:Policy:Wikimedia Access to Temporary Account IP Addresses Policy|Access to Temporary Account IP Addresses Policy]] via [[Special:Preferences]] or via the onboarding dialog which comes up after temporary accounts are deployed. '''Impact for administrators''' * It will be possible to block many abusers by just blocking their temporary accounts. A blocked person won't be able to create new temporary accounts quickly if the admin selects the [[mw:Autoblock|autoblock]] option. * It will still be possible to block an IP address or IP range. * Temporary accounts will not be retroactively applied to contributions made before the deployment. On [[Special:Contributions]], you will be able to see existing IP user contributions, but not new contributions made by temporary accounts on that IP address. Instead, you should use [[Special:IPContributions]] for this (see a video about IPContributions in a gallery below). '''Rules about IP information disclosure''' * Publicizing an IP address gained through TAIV access is [[wmf:Policy:Wikimedia Access to Temporary Account IP Addresses Policy#Disclosure|generally]] not allowed (e.g. {{!tq|~2025-12345-67 previously edited as 192.0.2.1}} or {{!tq|~2025-12345-67's IP address is 192.0.2.1}}). * Publicly linking a TA to another TA is allowed if "reasonably believed to be necessary". (e.g. {{tq|~2025-12345-67 and ~2025-12345-68 are likely the same person, so I am counting their reverts together toward [[WP:3RR|3RR]]}}, but not {{!tq|Hey ~2025-12345-68, you did some good editing as ~2025-12345-67}}) * See {{section link|Wikipedia:Temporary account IP viewer|What can and can't be said}} for more detailed guidelines. '''Useful tools for patrollers''' * It is possible to view if a user has opted-in to view temporary account IPs via the [[mw:Product Safety and Integrity/Anti-abuse signals/User Info|User Info card]], available in {{Preferences|Appearance|Advanced options|check={{int:checkuser-userinfocard-enable-preference-description}}}} ** This feature also makes it possible for anyone to see the approximate count of temporary accounts active on the same IP address range. * [[mw:Trust and Safety Product/Temporary Accounts/Access to IP#Special:IPContributions|Special:IPContributions]] allows viewing all edits and temporary accounts connected to a specific IP address or IP range. * Similarly, [[mw:Trust and Safety Product/Temporary Accounts/Access to IP#Special:GlobalContributions|Special:GlobalContributions]] supports global search for a given temporary account's activity. * The auto-reveal feature (see video below) allows users with the right permissions to automatically reveal all IP addresses for a limited time window. '''Videos''' Temporary Accounts demo - IP Contributions.webm|How to use Special:IPContributions Temporary Accounts demo - IP Auto-reveal.webm|How automatic IP reveal works Temporary Accounts demo - IP Information.webm|How to use IP Info Temporary Accounts demo - User Info Card.webm|How to use User Info '''Further information and discussion''' * For more information and discussion regarding this change, please see the announcement from the Wikimedia Foundation at {{section link|Wikipedia:Village pump (WMF)|Temporary accounts rollout}}. Most of this message was written by [[user:Mz7|Mz7]] ([[User:Mz7/sandbox/draft temp account massmessage|source]]). Hello! Voting in the '''[[WP:ACE2025|2025 Arbitration Committee elections]]''' is now open until 23:59 (UTC) on {{#time:l, j F Y|{{Arbitration Committee candidate/data|2025|end}}-1 day}}. All '''[[Wikipedia:Arbitration Committee Elections December 2025#Election timeline|eligible users]]''' are allowed to vote. Users with alternate accounts may only vote once. The [[WP:ARBCOM|Arbitration Committee]] is the panel of editors responsible for conducting the [[Wikipedia:Arbitration|Wikipedia arbitration process]]. It has the authority to impose binding solutions to disputes between editors, primarily for serious conduct disputes the community has been unable to resolve. This includes the authority to impose [[WP:BAN|site bans]], [[WP:TBAN|topic bans]], editing restrictions, and other measures needed to maintain our editing environment. The [[Wikipedia:Arbitration/Policy|arbitration policy]] describes the Committee's roles and responsibilities in greater detail. If you wish to participate in the 2025 election, please review [[Wikipedia:Arbitration Committee Elections December 2025/Candidates|the candidates]] and submit your choices on the '''[[Special:SecurePoll/vote/{{Arbitration Committee candidate/data|2025|poll}}|voting page]]'''. If you no longer wish to receive these messages, you may add {{tlx|NoACEMM}} to your user talk page. == Move of [[Emily]] == Hi there, it looks like you moved [[Emily]] to [[Emily (disambiguation)]] per a request at [[WP:RM/TR]] to make [[Emily (given name)]] the primary topic. First, conducting page moves to establish a primary topic, when the long-standing status quo was there being no primary topic for the term, is definitely [[WP:PCM|potentially controversial]] and should go through the normal RM discussion process and not be moved as a technical request. Page movers at TR should identify potentially controversial requests and contest them as such. Second, it looks like you only moved the disambiguation page, but didn't move [[Emily (given name)]] to [[Emily]], but instead just left [[Emily]] redirecting to [[Emily (disambiguation)]], which is [[WP:MALPLACED]]. Please be careful when executing moves that require [[WP:PAGEMOVER]] rights to ensure they are done properly. The issue is now properly being discussed at [[Talk:Emily_(given_name)#Requested_move_21_November_2025]]. Evidence submissions in this case will close on 14 December. ---- {{center|{{flatlist| * [[Wikipedia talk:Administrators' newsletter|Discuss this newsletter]] * [[Wikipedia:Administrators' newsletter/Subscribe|Subscribe]] * [[Wikipedia:Administrators' newsletter/Archive|Archive]] }}}}{{center|1=Sent by [[User:MediaWiki message delivery|MediaWiki message delivery]] ([[User talk:MediaWiki message delivery|talk]]) 16:20, 3 December 2024 (UTC)}} == Question from [[User:ZeHakim|ZeHakim]] (12:09, 15 December 2024) == i want to add old lebanese flags https://flagmakers.co.uk/media/trodxwqv/flag-of-lebanon-a-brief-history-download.pdf chat gpt wrote this === Ancient and Historical Flags === The region that is now Lebanon has had several flags throughout its history: File:Phoenician_Era_Flag.svg|{{FIAV|historical}} Flag of the Phoenician era (3000 BC – 200 AD), a blue-red bicolor flag. File:Tanukh_Tribe_Flag.svg|{{FIAV|historical}} Flag of the Tanukh tribe (200 AD – 400 AD), a white-blue-yellow-red-green vertical banded flag. File:Abbasid_Flag.svg|{{FIAV|historical}} Flag of the Abbasid Caliphate (750–1258), a plain black field. File:Kingdom_of_Jerusalem_Flag.svg|{{FIAV|historical}} Flag of the Kingdom of Jerusalem (1099–1291), a white field with a large gold cross and three smaller crosses. File:Maanid_Emirate_Flag.svg|{{FIAV|historical}} Flag of the Maanid Emirate (1119–1697), a white-red diagonal bicolor with a laurel wreath. File:Mamluk_Sultanate_Flag.svg|{{FIAV|historical}} Flag of the Mamluk Sultanate (1250–1517), an Egyptian yellow field with a left-facing crescent moon. File:Chehab_Emirate_Flag.svg|{{FIAV|historical}} Flag of the Chehab Emirate (1697–1842), a plain blue field with a right-facing crescent moon. === Military and Multinational Flags === Several military and international flags associated with Lebanon have also been used: File:Lebanese_Armed_Forces_Flag.svg|{{FIAV|military}} Flag of the Lebanese Armed Forces, a white-red diagonal bicolor with a coat of arms featuring crossed swords, a cedar, an anchor, and wings. File:First_Lebanese_Army_Flag.svg|{{FIAV|military}} First flag of the Lebanese Army, a red-white-red horizontal triband flag with a cedar tree in the center. File:Lebanese_Navy_Flag.svg|{{FIAV|military}} Flag of the Lebanese Navy, a plain white field with an anchor and an ancient ship in the center. File:Multinational_Force_Flag.svg|{{FIAV|international}} Flag of the Multinational Force in Lebanon, the flag of the Un --[[User:ZeHakim|ZeHakim]] ([[User talk:ZeHakim|talk]]) 12:09, 15 December 2024 (UTC) :Good evening Zahakim. [[User:LittleJerry|LittleJerry]] ([[User talk:LittleJerry|talk]]) 15:03, 11 June 2024 (UTC) :{{Re|LittleJerry}} You see, the issue with your perspective is that you ''only'' emphasize on the limblessness and body plan of snakes. To begin with, its scales are rougher than those of say, a Burmese python and it's much, much thinner. You may be right to say that all snakes have the "same" body plan, but so do lizards and many animal groups. Using a pit viper also has an additional drawback because, as you are well aware, not all snakes are green. {| style="float: right; border: 1px solid #BBB; background: #FFFFFF; |} Hello {{BASEPAGENAME}}, {| style="float: right; |- style="font-size: 86%;" |} [[File:NPP unreviewed article statistics as of April 2, 2024.svg|thumb|400px|New Page Review queue January to March 2024]] '''Backlog update:''' The October drive reduced the article backlog from 11,626 to 7,609 and the redirect backlog from 16,985 to 6,431! Congratulations to {{no ping|Schminnte}}, who led with over 2,300 points. Following that, New Page Patrol organized another backlog drive for articles in January 2024. The January drive started with 13,650 articles and reduced the backlog to 7,430 articles. Congratulations to {{no ping|JTtheOG}}, who achieved first place with 1,340 points in this drive. Looking at the graph, it seems like backlog drives are one of the only things keeping the backlog under control. Another backlog drive is being planned for May. Feel free to participate in the [[Wikipedia talk:New pages patrol/Reviewers#Next backlog drive|May backlog drive planning discussion]]. It's worth noting that both queues are gradually increasing again and are nearing 14,034 articles and 22,540 redirects. We encourage you to keep contributing, even if it's just a single patrol per day. Your support is greatly appreciated! '''2023 Awards''' [[File:New page reviewer of the year cup.svg|74px|left]] {{no ping|Onel5969}} won the 2023 cup with 17,761 article reviews last year - that's an average of nearly 50/day. There was one Platinum Award (10,000+ reviews), 2 Gold Awards (5000+ reviews), 6 Silver (2000+), 8 Bronze (1000+), 30 Iron (360+) and 70 more for the 100+ barnstar. {{no ping|Hey man im josh}} led on redirect reviews by clearing 36,175 of them. For the full details, see the [[Wikipedia:New pages patrol/Awards|Awards page]] and the [[Wikipedia:New pages patrol/Awards#NPP Hall of Fame|Hall of Fame]]. Congratulations everyone for their efforts in reviewing! '''WMF work on PageTriage:''' The [[:mw:Moderator Tools|WMF Moderator Tools team]] and volunteer software developers [[gerrit:c/mediawiki/extensions/PageTriage/+/966286|deployed the rewritten NewPagesFeed in October]], and then [[gerrit:c/mediawiki/extensions/PageTriage/+/967661|gave the NewPagesFeed a slight visual facelift in November]]. This concludes most major work to [[Special:NewPagesFeed]], and most major work by the WMF Moderator Tools team, who [[Wikipedia:Page Curation/2023 Moderator Tools project#October 20, 2023: Final update!|wrapped up their major work on PageTriage in October]]. The WMF Moderator Tools team and volunteer software developers will continue small work on PageTriage as time permits. '''Recruitment:''' A couple of the coordinators have been inviting editors to become reviewers, via mass-messages to their talk pages. If you know someone who you'd think would make a good reviewer, then a personal invitation to them would be great. Additionally, if there are Wikiprojects that you are active on, then you can add a post there asking participants to join NPP. Please be careful not to double invite folks that have already been invited. '''Reviewing tip:''' Reviewers who prefer to patrol new pages within their most familiar subjects can use the regularly updated [https://nppbrowser.toolforge.org/ NPP Browser] tool. {{refbegin}} '''Reminders:''' * You can access live chat with patrollers on the [https://discordapp.com/invite/heF3xPu New Pages Patrol Discord]. * Consider adding [[Wikipedia talk:New pages patrol/Reviewers|the project discussion page]] to your watchlist. * To opt out of future mailings, please remove yourself [[Wikipedia:New pages patrol/Reviewers/Newsletter list|here]]. {{refend}}

That said and agreed, these are stable links to fully open-access resources (the PDF file directly, for [https://www.toaep.org/nas-pdf/4-bergsmo-dittrich example]) and are not under [[Wikipedia:External links#Links normally to be avoided|those]] which should be avoided. [[User:AAngotti01|AAngotti01]] ([[User talk:AAngotti01|talk]]) 10:16, 11 April 2024 (UTC) : What follows is my opinion, not a statement of Wikipedia policy. You have listed a number of books published by TOAEP, and have provided a link to their website. I believe that is sufficient for any interested reader to access the material published by TOAEP. Linking to the individual publications as part of a citation for that publication would be excessive and unnecessary. Such excessive linking is often used as a [[search engine optimization]] strategy, as search engines give higher priority to sites that have more in-links, but that does not work with Wikipedia since Wikipedia expressly codes their pages to cause search engines not to count links as in-links to other sites. I'm not claiming that you are engaged in an SEO strategy, but excessive linking can be seen as an attempt at that and will be reverted anyway. [[File:Copyeditors progress.png|right|thumb]] Hello and welcome to the April 2024 newsletter, a quarterly digest of Guild activities since [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Guild of Copy Editors/Newsletters/December 2023|December]]. Don't forget you can unsubscribe at any time; see below. We extend a warm welcome to all of our new members. We wish you all happy copy-editing. '''[[Wikipedia:WikiProject Guild of Copy Editors/Coordinators/2024/1|Election results]]:''' In our December 2023 coordinator election, Zippybonzo stepped down as coordinator; we thank them for their service. Incumbents Dhtwiki and Miniapolis were reelected coordinators, and Wracking was newly elected coordinator, to serve through 30 June. Nominations for our mid-year [[Wikipedia:WikiProject_Guild_of_Copy_Editors/Coordinators#How_are_we_selected?|Election of Coordinators]] will open on 1 June (UTC). '''Drive:''' 46 editors signed up for our [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Guild of Copy Editors/Backlog elimination drives/January 2024|'''January Backlog Elimination Drive''']], 32 of whom claimed at least one copy-edit. Between them, they copy-edited 289 articles totaling 626,729 words. Barnstars awarded are [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Guild of Copy Editors/Backlog elimination drives/January 2024/Barnstars|'''here''']]. '''Blitz:''' 23 editors signed up for our [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Guild of Copy Editors/Blitzes/February 2024|'''February Copy Editing Blitz''']]. 18 claimed at least one copy-edit and between them, they copy-edited 100,293 words in 32 articles. Barnstars awarded are [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Guild of Copy Editors/Blitzes/February 2024/Barnstars|'''here''']]. '''Drive:''' 53 editors signed up for our [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Guild of Copy Editors/Backlog elimination drives/March 2024|'''March Backlog Elimination Drive''']], 34 of whom claimed at least one copy-edit. Between them, they copy-edited 300 articles totaling 587,828 words. Barnstars awarded are [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Guild of Copy Editors/Backlog elimination drives/March 2024/Barnstars|'''here''']]. '''Blitz:''' Sign up for our [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Guild of Copy Editors/Blitzes/April 2024|'''April Copy Editing Blitz''']], which runs from 14 to 20 April. Barnstars will be awarded [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Guild of Copy Editors/Blitzes/April 2024/Barnstars|'''here''']]. '''Progress report:''' As of 23:17, 11 April 2024 (UTC), GOCE copyeditors have processed 109 requests since 1 January 2024, and the backlog stands at 2,480 articles. Thank you all again for your participation; we wouldn't be able to achieve what we have without you! Cheers from {{noping|Baffle gab1978}} and your GOCE coordinators {{noping|Dhtwiki|Miniapolis|Wracking}}. {{center | To discontinue receiving GOCE newsletters, please remove your name from [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Guild of Copy Editors/Mailing List|our mailing list]]. }} |} == Christianity in Iraq == Hello Dan! I saw the warring on the Christianity in Iraq page and came here to thank you with your patience in the talk section. Thus for now I will try to conduct my research whilst not straying too far from my university assignments. Once again, I appreciate your pateince! [[User:WikiDan61|WikiDan61]][[User talk:WikiDan61|ChatMe!]][[Special:Contributions/WikiDan61|ReadMe!!]] 16:14, 30 May 2024 (UTC) == Sorry for reverting your edit fam == The revert is on Kosovo national stadium they have no prof of copyright [[User:Itsbn007|Itsbn007]] ([[User talk:Itsbn007|talk]]) 13:56, 31 May 2024 (UTC) == About Joe Donnelly == Hey, WikiDan I just wanted to talk to you about one of the reasons why the Article was denied. Though taking a second look at my article, all the criticisms and problems you had pointed out were very reasonable and true. In fact, I realized I'd forgotten to put a very important source to my research for the references. There was one reason however I would like to point out. That being, I'm in no way related to the individual at hand. I know some parts in the article sound like I'm meat-riding the guy. I just wanted to give some context on why I decided to make the acticle in the first place. I live in a small Canadian town and a while ago I went into my local used book store, and I saw one of Joe Donnelly's books. I wanted to know a little more about the guy so I looked him up online, and found bearly anything. And I thought of how sad it was that this guy didn't have any information about himself out there. So I got an Idea in my head. Why not make a Wikipedia article on this guy, so that all the information about him could be in one spot? So yeah that's all I had to say. For the other reasons, I’ll try to clean it up and bring it up to standards. (I've added several comments in my userspace version where this is especially problematic.) == Guild of Copy Editors June 2024 Newsletter == {| style="position: relative; margin-left: 2em; margin-right: 2em; padding: 0.5em 1em; background-color: #dfeff3; border: 2px solid #bddff2; border-color: rgba( 109, 193, 240, 0.75 ); border-radius: 8px; box-shadow: 8px 8px 12px rgba( 0, 0, 0, 0.7 );" | '''[[WP:GOCE|Guild of Copy Editors]] June 2024 Newsletter'''
[[File:Writing Magnifying.PNG|100px|link=]]
[[File:Copyeditors progress.png|right|thumb]] Hello and welcome to the June 2024 newsletter, a quarterly-ish digest of Guild activities since April. Don't forget you can unsubscribe at any time; see below. '''Election news:''' Wanted: new Guild coordinators! If you value and enjoy the GOCE, why not help out behind the scenes? Nominations for our [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Guild of Copy Editors/Coordinators/2024/2|mid-year coordinator election]] are now open until 23:59 on 15 June (UTC). Self-nominations are welcome. Voting commences at 00:01 on 16 June and continues until 23:50 on 30 June. Results will be announced at the election page. '''Blitz:''' Nine of the fourteen editors who signed up for the [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Guild of Copy Editors/Blitzes/April_2024|'''April 2024 Copy Editing Blitz''']] copy edited at least one article. Between them, they copy edited 55,853 words comprising twenty articles. Barnstars awarded are available [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Guild of Copy Editors/Blitzes/April_2024/Barnstars|'''here''']]. '''Drive:''' 58 editors signed up for our [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Guild of Copy Editors/Backlog elimination drives/May_2024|'''May 2024 Backlog Elimination Drive''']] and 33 of those completed at least one copy edit. 251 articles and 475,952 words were copy edited. Barnstars awarded are [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Guild of Copy Editors/Backlog elimination drives/May_2024/Barnstars|'''here''']]. '''Blitz:''' Our [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Guild of Copy Editors/Blitzes/June_2024|'''June 2024 Copy Editing Blitz''']] will begin on 16 June and finish on 22 June. Barnstars awarded will be posted [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Guild of Copy Editors/Blitzes/June_2024/Barnstars|'''here''']]. '''Progress report:''' As of 05:23, 8 June 2024 (UTC) , GOCE copyeditors have completed 161 requests since 1 January and the backlog stands at 2,779 articles. Thank you all again for your participation; we wouldn't be able to achieve what we have without you! Cheers from {{noping|Baffle gab1978}} and your GOCE coordinators {{noping|Dhtwiki|Miniapolis|Wracking}}. {{center | To discontinue receiving GOCE newsletters, please remove your name from [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Guild of Copy Editors/Mailing List|our mailing list]]. }} |} == Request to Rename Wikipedia Page - Jos Alukkas & Sons == Dear Wikipedia Editors, I am writing to request a rename of the Wikipedia page currently titled "Jos Alukkas & Sons" to "Jos Alukkas." Our company's registered brand name is "Jos Alukkas," and we have recently standardized our branding across all platforms to reflect this. The existing Wikipedia page, established 11 years ago, uses the older "Jos Alukkas & Sons" title. To avoid confusion and maintain consistency with our current branding, we believe renaming the page to "Jos Alukkas" would be more accurate and user-friendly for the public. We understand the process for renaming pages might involve certain guidelines. We are happy to follow those guidelines and provide any necessary information to facilitate this change. Additionally, we have created a new page titled "Jos Alukkas" and implemented a redirect from the old "Jos Alukkas & Sons" page to the new one. This ensures users searching for either term will land on the correct information. Thank you for your time and consideration. We appreciate your help in keeping Wikipedia's information accurate and up-to-date. Sincerely, Deepu Antony Brand Manager - Jos Alukkas [[User:Deepu3584|Deepu3584]] ([[User talk:Deepu3584|talk]]) 12:40, 18 June 2024 (UTC) : {{reply|Depu3684}} Due to your [[WP:COI|conflict of interest]], you should avoid editing either [[Jos Alukkas & Sons]] or [[Jos Alukkas]] directly. [[File:Copyeditors progress.png|right|thumb]] Hello and welcome to the September newsletter, a quarterly digest of Guild activities since June. Don't forget you can unsubscribe at any time; see below. '''Election news:''' Project coordinators play an important role in our WikiProject. Following the [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Guild of Copy Editors/Coordinators/2024/2|mid-year Election of Coordinators]], we welcomed {{noping|Mox Eden}} to the coordinator team. {{noping|Dhtwiki}} remains as Lead Coordinator, and {{noping|Miniapolis|Wracking}} returned as assistant coordinators. If you'd like to help out behind the scenes, please consider taking part in our December election – watchlist our [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Guild of Copy Editors|ombox]] for updates. Information about the role of coordinators can be found [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Guild of Copy Editors/Coordinators|here]]. '''Blitz:''' 13 of the 24 editors who signed up for the [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Guild of Copy Editors/Blitzes/June 2024|June 2024 Copy Editing Blitz]] copy edited at least one article. Between them, they copy edited 169,404 words comprising 41 articles. Barnstars awarded are [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Guild of Copy Editors/Blitzes/June 2024/Barnstars|here]]. '''Drive:''' 38 of the 59 editors who signed up for the [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Guild of Copy Editors/Backlog elimination drives/July_2024|July 2024 Backlog Elimination Drive]] copy edited at least one article. Between them, they copy edited 482,133 words comprising 293 articles. Barnstars awarded are [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Guild of Copy Editors/Backlog elimination drives/July_2024/Barnstars|here]]. '''Blitz:''' 10 of the 15 editors who signed up for the [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Guild of Copy Editors/Blitzes/August 2024|August 2024 Copy Editing Blitz]] copy edited at least one article. Between them, they copy edited 71,294 words comprising 31 articles. Barnstars awarded are [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Guild of Copy Editors/Blitzes/August 2024/Barnstars|here]]. '''Drive:''' [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Guild of Copy Editors/Backlog elimination drives/September_2024#Signing_up|'''Sign up here''']] to earn barnstars in our month-long, in-progress September Backlog Elimination Drive. '''Progress report:''' As of 05:14, 11 September 2024 (UTC), GOCE copyeditors have processed 233 [[WP:GOCER|requests]] since 1 January, and the backlog of tagged articles stands at 2,824 articles. Thank you all again for your participation; we wouldn't be able to achieve what we do without you! ==Your material in the Maharishi article== I support what you want to see retained in the Maharishi Mahesh Yogi article about his relations with various young women. I know some people object because many (in the West, at least) may feel his behaviour was inappropriate. I've arrived at a non-judgmental view. I learned TM as well as other methods of meditation, and I utilize them, knowing that I benefit thereby. I regard MMY as a teacher & promoter of meditation. It's not my personal business whether or not he kept brahmachari vows. The way I look at the situation is, yes he may have seduced many women. But, as far as I know, the sex was consensual and the women were of majority age. If it were established there were any instances where this was not the case, then I'd think there ''would'' be a rationale for oppprobrium—and that would certainly alter the relevant content. Otherwise, I can't understand grounds for complaints about retaining this section in the article. Another source in support of the sexuality aspect is Paul Mason's biography of MMY. So my feeling is the TM organization(s) and followers of MMY should stay out of demanding that the facts be removed.[[User:Joel Russ|Joel Russ]] ([[User talk:Joel Russ|talk]]) 20:32, 2 October 2024 (UTC) : {{reply|Joel Russ}} Per [[WP:NPOV|Wikipedia's neutrality policy]], we don't engage in opprobrium here. We simply report the facts as we find them in [[WP:RS|reliable sources]]. However, the Maharishi's sexual activity is controversial because it directly contravened his own instruction given to his followers, and his actions involving Mia Farrow seemed anything but consensual. The material is a small part of the overall biography, and seems to have been given proper [[WP:UNDUE|weight]] in the article. [[User:WikiDan61|WikiDan61]][[User talk:WikiDan61|ChatMe!]][[Special:Contributions/WikiDan61|ReadMe!!]] 13:26, 3 October 2024 (UTC) ::Makes sense.[[User:Joel Russ|Joel Russ]] ([[User talk:Joel Russ|talk]]) 03:12, 4 October 2024 (UTC) == Alpha Kappa Alpha == I am an authorized member of Alpha Kappa Alpha Sorority, Incorporated to update this wikipedia page as it has a massive amount of inaccurate information. All of the information added will come directly from the international website, aka1908.com. Therefore, the citation of all updated information will come from there. Please advise, thanks. I disagree with some of your comments about the additions being trivial, in the UK the Permanent Secretary is the highest rank in the Civil Service and thus a comment from that position is in effect official government commentary, a bit like a Minister of State stating something. In fact anything stated has to be approved, thus comments made are important to pull together as it paints a record of what was going on at the highest echelons of UK government at that time. The biggest challenge i have is a large number of images as they are not online anywhere. [[User:DavidPolymath|DavidPolymath]] ([[User talk:DavidPolymath|talk]]) 22:58, 13 November 2024 (UTC) : {{reply|DavidPolymath}} The fact that Parker was Permanent Secretary was already covered in the article. In other words, he did his job. There is nothing remarkable about any of these deeds other than the fact that someone captured a moment in a photo, or noted the event in a newspaper article. And his involvement in a private swimming club is also trivial. As Parker's grandson, your [[WP:COI|personal feelings]] about the subject may cloud your judgement about what is proper to include in the article. I have put no feelings into this piece just 100% pure facts, the relative importance facts is debatable but as they are all references they are all reasonable. [[File:Copyeditors progress.png|thumb]] Hello, and welcome to the December newsletter, a quarterly digest of Guild activities since September. If you no longer want this newsletter, you can unsubscribe at any time; see below. If you'd like to be notified of upcoming drives and blitzes, and other GOCE activities, the best method is to add our [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Guild of Copy Editors/Ombox|announcements box]] to your watchlist. '''Election news:''' The Guild's coordinators play an important role in the WikiProject, making sure nearly everything runs smoothly and on time. Editors in good standing (unblocked and without sanctions) are invited to [[Wikipedia:WikiProject_Guild_of_Copy_Editors/Coordinators/2025/1|'''nominate themselves or another editor to be a Guild coordinator''']] (with their permission, of course) until 23:59 on 15 December (UTC). The voting phase begins at 00:01 on 16 December and runs until 23:59 on 31 December. Questions may be asked of candidates at any stage in the process. Elected coordinators will serve a six-month term from 1 January through 30 June. '''Drive:''' In our [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Guild of Copy Editors/Backlog elimination drives/September_2024|'''September Backlog Elimination Drive''']], 67 editors signed up, 39 completed at least one copy edit, and between them they edited 682,696 words comprising 507 articles. Barnstars awarded are [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Guild of Copy Editors/Backlog elimination drives/September_2024/Barnstars|'''here''']]. '''Blitz:''' The [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Guild of Copy Editors/Blitzes/October_2024|'''October Copy Editing Blitz''']] saw 16 editors sign-up, 15 of whom completed at least one copy edit. They edited 76,776 words comprising 35 articles. Barnstars awarded are [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Guild of Copy Editors/Blitzes/October_2024/Barnstars|'''here''']]. '''Drive:''' In our [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Guild of Copy Editors/Backlog elimination drives/November_2024|'''November Backlog Elimination Drive''']], 432,320 words in 151 articles were copy edited. Of the 54 users who signed up, 33 copy edited at least one article. Barnstars awarded are posted [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Guild of Copy Editors/Backlog elimination drives/November_2024/Barnstars|'''here''']]. '''Blitz:''' The December Blitz will begin at 00:00 on 15 December (UTC) and will end on 21 December at 23:59. [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Guild of Copy Editors/Blitzes/December_2024#Signing_up|'''Sign up here''']]. Barnstars awarded will be posted [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Guild of Copy Editors/Blitzes/December_2024/Barnstars|'''here''']]. '''Progress report:''' As of 22:12, 7 December 2024 (UTC), GOCE copy editors have completed 333 requests since 1 January, and the backlog of tagged articles stands at 2,401 articles. Thank you all again for your participation; we wouldn't be able to achieve what we have without you! Cheers from your GOCE coordinators, {{noping|Dhtwiki|Miniapolis|Mox Eden|Wracking}}. {{center | To stop receiving GOCE newsletters, please remove your name from [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Guild of Copy Editors/Mailing List|our mailing list]]. }} |}

Delivered by ''[[User:SDZeroBot|SDZeroBot]]'' ([[User talk:SDZeroBot|talk]]) 01:01, 12 March 2025 (UTC) == Removing Before Jan Sloot == '''Hi WikiDan61,''' I wanted to discuss Jan Sloot's final days, especially the claims he made about receiving death threats before his death in 1999. According to his son, Ben Sloot, and his business associate Roel Pieper, Jan frequently mentioned securing important materials in a safe due to these threats. Pieper even mentioned that Jan often said, ''"Every week I put something somewhere. In a safe."'' Interestingly, after his death, the source code for his Sloot Digital Coding System was said to have been locked away in a safe at ABN Amro, but when the safe was opened, only early prototypes were found, and the final code was nowhere to be found. This has fueled speculation about whether the full system was ever secured and what really happened to it. The story about his threats and the mysterious disappearance of his final work has always intrigued me, especially considering the significance of his invention. If you're interested, I’ve added some links that provide additional information on the subject: * [https://jansloot.telcomsoft.nl/Sources-1/Verhaal/Verhaal.htm Sloot's Story] * [https://www.gids.nl/techno/jan-sloot.html More on Jan Sloot] Look up the part where it says, ''"Hij vertelde tegen iedereen dat hij al eens met de dood bedreigd was."'' (In English: "He told everyone that he had already received death threats.") This information isn’t false—you can verify it through the links I’ve provided. If you're unable to read Dutch, I’d be happy to translate the text or the whole story for you. Let me know your thoughts on this, or if you have any insights into the mysterious disappearance of his work! [[User:NewAgent2025|NewAgent2025]] ([[User talk:NewAgent2025|talk]]) 06:08, 27 March 2025 (UTC) : {{reply|NewAgent2025}} Documents that a journalist uploaded to their own private website are also not [[WP:RS|reliable]]. If these were legitimate documents, why was there no reporting in reliable sources about the threats? You have explicitly stated in your edits that these details fueled speculation about the nature of Sloot's death. I would offer an alternative interpretation: Sloot was something of a [[crackpot index|crackpot]], and such people are prone to paranoia. Sloot thought he was on to something big, and therefore thought the world was out to get him to prevent his big reveal. His own thoughts on the matter, reported by himself and by a reporter (but never published) are not reliable sources. We should not allow Sloot's conspiracy theory to become Wikipedia fact. [[File:Copyeditors progress.png|right|thumb]] Hello and welcome to the April 2025 newsletter, a quarterly digest of Guild activities since [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Guild of Copy Editors/Newsletters/December 2024|December]]. Don't forget you can unsubscribe at any time; see below. We extend a warm welcome to all of our new members. We wish you all happy copy-editing. '''[[Wikipedia:WikiProject Guild of Copy Editors/Coordinators/2025/1|Election results]]:''' In our December 2025 coordinator election, Wracking stepped down as coordinator; we thank them for their service. Incumbents Dhtwiki, Miniapolis, and Mox Eden were reelected coordinators, and IQR and WikiEditor5678910 were newly elected coordinator, to serve through 30 June. Nominations for our mid-year [[Wikipedia:WikiProject_Guild_of_Copy_Editors/Coordinators#How_are_we_selected?|Election of Coordinators]] will open on 1 June (UTC). '''Drive:''' 55 editors signed up for our [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Guild of Copy Editors/Backlog elimination drives/January 2025|'''January Backlog Elimination Drive''']] 33 claimed at least one copy-edit and copy-edited 611,404 words in 237 articles. Barnstars awarded are [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Guild of Copy Editors/Backlog elimination drives/January 2025/Barnstars|'''here''']]. '''Blitz:''' 14 editors signed up for our [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Guild of Copy Editors/Blitzes/February 2025|'''February Copy Editing Blitz''']]. 10 claimed at least one copy-edit and copy-edited 46,749 words in 18 articles. Barnstars awarded are [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Guild of Copy Editors/Blitzes/February 2025/Barnstars|'''here''']]. '''Drive:''' 47 editors signed up for our [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Guild of Copy Editors/Backlog elimination drives/March 2025|'''March Backlog Elimination Drive''']]. 28 claimed at least one copy-edit and copy-edited 479,172 words in 207 articles. Barnstars awarded are [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Guild of Copy Editors/Backlog elimination drives/March 2025/Barnstars|'''here''']]. '''Blitz:''' Sign up for our [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Guild of Copy Editors/Blitzes/April 2025|'''April Copy Editing Blitz''']], which runs from 13 to 19 April. Barnstars will be awarded [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Guild of Copy Editors/Blitzes/April 2025/Barnstars|'''here''']]. '''Progress report:''' As of 9:45, 12 April 2024 (UTC), GOCE copyeditors have processed 89 requests since 1 January 2024, and the backlog stands at 2,264 articles. Thank you all again for your participation; we wouldn't be able to achieve what we have without you! Cheers from your GOCE coordinators {{noping|Dhtwiki|IQR|Miniapolis|Mox Eden|WikiEditor5678910}}. {{center | To discontinue receiving GOCE newsletters, please remove your name from [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Guild of Copy Editors/Mailing List|our mailing list]]. }} |} [[User:MediaWiki message delivery|MediaWiki message delivery]] ([[User talk:MediaWiki message delivery|talk]]) 22:55, 12 April 2025 (UTC) == [[Commercial Bank of Dubai]] == I noticed that you had taken an interest in the edits of the latest editor. Schechter Poultry Corp. v. United States|A.L.A. Schechter Poultry Corp. v. United States,]] which is widely considered one of the most significant Supreme Court decisions in American history. The S.C. State Fair is a 501c3 that gives over a half million dollars to scholarships each year. They are 156 years old and the biggest event in South Carolina. A shame that the page has been neglected and a group of us are trying to see how to update it. Please give advice on how this can happen or are we just out of luck? Then we will stop. [[User:MetsHoffman|MetsHoffman]] ([[User talk:MetsHoffman|talk]]) 12:29, 3 May 2025 (UTC) : {{reply|MetsHoffman}} What you're describing is called [[WP:MEAT|meat puppetry]]: when a blocked editor enlists the help of other editors to perform the edits that got them blocked in the first place. There are two basic problems at play here: :# The material that the SC State Fair employee tried to add was essentially copied from the Fair's history page. Attempts were made to obfuscate that copying (this is called [[WP:PARAPHRASE|close paraphrasing]], and it still violates copyright guidelines). The state fair editor was blocked, and then the alternate account they created was also blocked. :# The material represent what the Fair wants to say about itself. But this is not Wikipedia's purpose. The Fair already has its own website to say what it wants about itself. Wikipedia's purpose is to report what indepdendent [[WP:RS|reliable sources]] have said about the fair. So, creating a large swath of history based solely on the Fair's own history page doesn't really serve Wikipedia's purposes. -- [[User:WikiDan61|WikiDan61]][[User talk:WikiDan61|ChatMe!]][[Special:Contributions/WikiDan61|ReadMe!!]] 13:31, 4 May 2025 (UTC) :Okay thank you. That is interesting because we've always used Wikipedia personally as a way to get to know about a subject without researching their websites. I guess like the old fashioned Encylopedia but written by everyday people who know. [[User:MetsHoffman|MetsHoffman]] ([[User talk:MetsHoffman|talk]]) 22:05, 4 May 2025 (UTC) == Your kind attention required == Dear Wikipedia Editor(s), I am writing to respectfully request a review and potential reinstatement of my recent edits to the Wikipedia page for the singer Jithin Raj. However, in this specific instance, the extensive changes made by others have essentially replaced the original subject of the page. [[User:WikiDan61|WikiDan61]][[User talk:WikiDan61|ChatMe!]][[Special:Contributions/WikiDan61|ReadMe!!]] 20:23, 5 May 2025 (UTC) == Hi == How are you [[Special:Contributions/217.165.43.56|217.165.43.56]] ([[User talk:217.165.43.56|talk]]) 02:51, 19 May 2025 (UTC) == Why - james Bond == I had a different argument; figured the issue was "in office" so switched with "in role". Makes perfect sense, but alas, shot down. [[User:DoubleDouble7|DoubleDouble7]] ([[User talk:DoubleDouble7|talk]]) 13:44, 29 May 2025 (UTC) == Tony Harvey (freemason) == In answer to your queries. In accordance with Wikipedia naming guidelines -[[Wikipedia:Naming conventions (people)]] , when disambiguating articles on people with the same name the practice is to use the title of what they are most notable for to differentiate between the individuals, such as politician, football, author etc. Those are provided in brackets following the name and are in lower case, such as [[John Boswell (freemason)]]. There is a whole set of guidelines in relation to capitalisation of words, titles if you want to research those further. The practice when disambiguating articles is to use the field that they are most notable for, not multiple fields (ie scout & freemason) - noting wherever possible avoid using an ampersand (&) in the title. I hope that this clarifies my changes for you, if you have any additional questions let me know. [[File:Writing Magnifying.PNG|100px|link=]]
[[File:Copyeditors progress.png|right|thumb]] Hello and welcome to the June 2025 newsletter, a quarterly-ish digest of Guild activities since April. Don't forget you can unsubscribe at any time; see below. '''Hall of Fame''': Congratulations to {{u|Dhtwiki}} for {{their|Dhtwiki}} well-deserved addition to the [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Guild of Copy Editors/GOCE Hall of Fame|Hall of Fame]] last month, and thanks to {{u|GoldRomean}} for the nomination. '''Election news:''' Voting in the [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Guild of Copy Editors/Coordinators/2025/2|'''mid-year coordinator election''']] ends at 23:59 on 30 June. Results will be announced at the election page. '''April Blitz:''' 14 of the 25 editors who signed up for the [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Guild of Copy Editors/Blitzes/April_2025|'''April 2025 Copy Editing Blitz''']] copy edited 92,769 words in 30 articles. Barnstars awarded are available [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Guild of Copy Editors/Blitzes/April_2025/Barnstars|'''here''']]. '''May Drive:''' 31 of the 54 editors who signed up for the [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Guild of Copy Editors/Backlog elimination drives/May_2025|May 2025 Backlog Elimination Drive]] copy edited 384,392 words in 216 articles. Barnstars awarded are [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Guild of Copy Editors/Backlog elimination drives/May_2025/Barnstars|'''here''']]. '''June Blitz:''' 10 of the 12 editors who signed up for the [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Guild of Copy Editors/Blitzes/June_2025|'''June 2025 Copy Editing Blitz''']] copy edited 26,652 words in 13 articles. Barnstars awarded are [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Guild of Copy Editors/Blitzes/June_2025/Barnstars|'''here''']]. '''July Drive:''' Our [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Guild of Copy Editors/Backlog elimination drives/July_2025|'''July 2025 Backlog Elimination Drive''']] will begin on 1 July and finish on 31 July. Barnstars awarded will be posted [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Guild of Copy Editors/Backlog elimination drives/July_2025/Barnstars|'''here''']]. '''Progress report:''' As of 02:30, 30 June 2025 (UTC), GOCE copyeditors have completed 148 requests since 1 January, and the backlog stands at 2,270 articles. Thank you all again for your participation; we wouldn't be able to achieve what we have without you! Cheers from your GOCE coordinators. {{center | To discontinue receiving GOCE newsletters, please remove your name from [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Guild of Copy Editors/Mailing List|our mailing list]]. }} |} [[User:MediaWiki message delivery|MediaWiki message delivery]] ([[User talk:MediaWiki message delivery|talk]]) 23:19, 30 June 2025 (UTC) == Gedo today is part of Somalia == [[User:Xaliimo123|Xaliimo123]] ([[User talk:Xaliimo123|talk]]) 11:41, 5 August 2025 (UTC) : {{reply|Xaliimo123}} Please read the reply I posted to you at [[User talk:PhilKnight]]. [[User:WikiDan61|WikiDan61]][[User talk:WikiDan61|ChatMe!]][[Special:Contributions/WikiDan61|ReadMe!!]] 11:43, 5 August 2025 (UTC) == Gedo == @WikiDan61 Dear Wikipedia Administrator, I hope this message finds you well. However, I would like to clarify that my edits were based entirely on recent, verifiable, and reliable sources from July and August 2025, reflecting the ongoing political and military developments in the Gedo region—specifically the federal government’s control of Beled-Xaawo and related clashes with Jubbaland forces. To ensure compliance with Wikipedia’s sourcing standards, I have included multiple citations from reputable media outlets that document the following: The arrival of federal government-appointed security commanders in Beled-Xaawo. Armed conflict between federal and Jubbaland-aligned forces. Statements from the Somali federal cabinet confirming federal military presence in Gedo. Confirmation of control shifts in Beled-Xaawo and the political significance of these actions. Below are a few reliable sources supporting the edits: 1. Mustaqbal Media (31 July 2025) – Federal forces take control of Beled-Xaawo Link 4. Hiiraan Online (28 July 2025) – Clashes erupt in Gedo amid federal–Jubbaland struggle Link These articles explicitly verify the claims made in the updated version of the Gedo article, particularly the shift in administrative and security control over Beled-Xaawo, a topic highly relevant to the article’s subject matter. I respectfully request a review of the reversion and reconsideration of the content based on the verified sources provided above. AllAfrica / Shabelle Media (22 July 2025) 📰 "Heavy fighting erupts in Beled-Hawo between Somali govt forces and Jubbaland troops" 🔗 https://allafrica.com/stories/202507220507.html 3. Mustaqbal Media (31 July 2025) 📰 "Federal government forces take control of Beled-Hawo after fierce clashes with Jubaland troops" 🔗 https://mustaqbalmedia.net/en/federal-government-forces-take-control-of-beled-hawo-after-fierce-clashes-with-jubaland-troops 4. Hiiraan Online (28 July 2025) 📰 "Clashes erupt in Somalia's Gedo region amid federal–Jubbaland power struggle" 🔗 https://hiiraan.com/news4/2025/July/202355/clashes_erupt_in_somalia_s_gedo_region_amid_federal_jubbaland_power_struggle.aspx [[User:Xaliimo123|Xaliimo123]] ([[User talk:Xaliimo123|talk]]) 12:47, 5 August 2025 (UTC) : {{reply|Xaliimo123}} The articles you have cited here ''all'' refer to Gedo as a region, not a state. == Event coordinator granted == [[File:Wikipedia Event coordinator.svg|right|130px]] After reviewing your request for the [[Wikipedia:Event coordinator|"eventcoordinator" permission]], I have temporarily enabled the flag on your account until 2025-05-01. Keep in mind these things: *The event coordinator right removes the limit on the maximum number of new accounts that can be created in a 24-hour period. *The event coordinator right allows you to ''temporarily'' add the "[[Wikipedia:Confirmed users|confirmed]]" permission to newly created accounts. You should not grant this for more than 10 days. *The event coordinator right is not a status symbol. If it remains unused, it is likely to be removed. Abuse of the event coordinator right will result in its removal by an administrator. If you no longer require the right, let me know, or ask any other administrator. Drop a note on my talk page if you run into troubles or have any questions about appropriate/inappropriate use of the event coordinator right. Happy editing! [[File:Writing Magnifying.PNG|100px|link=]]
[[File:Copyeditors progress.png|right|thumb]] Hello and welcome to the September newsletter, a quarterly digest of Guild activities since June. '''Election news:''' Project coordinators play an important role in our WikiProject. Following the [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Guild of Copy Editors/Coordinators/2025/2|mid-year Election of Coordinators]], we welcomed {{noping|GoldRomean}} to the coordinator team. {{noping|Dhtwiki}} remains as lead coordinator, and {{noping|Miniapolis|Mox Eden}} return as coordinators. If you'd like to help out behind the scenes, please consider taking part in our December election – watchlist our [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Guild of Copy Editors|ombox]] for updates. Information about the role of coordinators can be found [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Guild of Copy Editors/Coordinators|here]]. '''June 2025 blitz:''' 10 of the 12 editors who signed up for the [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Guild of Copy Editors/Blitzes/June 2025|June 2025 Copy Editing Blitz]] copy edited a total of 26,652 words comprising 13 articles. Barnstars awarded are [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Guild of Copy Editors/Blitzes/June 2025/Barnstars|here]]. '''July 2025 drive:''' 30 of the 54 editors who signed up for the [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Guild of Copy Editors/Backlog elimination drives/July_2025|July 2025 Backlog Elimination Drive]] copy edited a total of 379,557 words comprising 151 articles. Barnstars awarded are [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Guild of Copy Editors/Backlog elimination drives/July_2025/Barnstars|here]]. '''August 2025 Blitz:''' 11 of the 17 editors who signed up for the [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Guild of Copy Editors/Blitzes/August 2025|August 2025 Copy Editing Blitz]] copy edited a total of 65,601 words comprising 25 articles. Barnstars awarded are [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Guild of Copy Editors/Blitzes/August 2025/Barnstars|here]]. '''September 2025 Drive:''' [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Guild of Copy Editors/Backlog elimination drives/September_2025#Signing_up|'''Sign up here''']] to earn barnstars in our month-long, in-progress September Backlog Elimination Drive. '''Progress report:''' As of 06:43, 20 September 2025 (UTC), GOCE copyeditors have processed 222 [[WP:GOCER|requests]] since 1 January, and the backlog of tagged articles stands at 2,010 articles. Thank you all again for your participation; we wouldn't be able to achieve what we do without you! Cheers from your GOCE coordinators. {{center | To discontinue receiving GOCE newsletters, please remove your name from [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Guild of Copy Editors/Mailing List|our mailing list]]. }} |} [[User:MediaWiki message delivery|MediaWiki message delivery]] ([[User talk:MediaWiki message delivery|talk]]) 23:46, 20 September 2025 (UTC) == Wikidata weekly summary #698 ==

''' Events ''' * [[d:Special:MyLanguage/Wikidata:Events|Upcoming events]]: ** [[Event:Exploring Seediq: A Journey through Translation and Linked Data Documentation|Exploring Seediq: A Journey through Translation and Linked Data Documentation]] 25 October .This event will showcase the Seediq communities’ translation of the OCLC white paper on Wikibase, followed by an overview of the full paper. It will conclude with an open discussion on the lessons learned during the translation project and broader reflections on linked data. ** [[d:Event:Govdirectory US office hour September 2025|Govdirectory US office hour]], 25 September ** FREE virtual 1hr Wiki webinar focusing on plant biodiversity. Open to anyone wanting to improve their digital outreach skills by enriching Wikidata. Tuesday 14th of October at 3pm NZST. [https://tepapa.zoom.us/meeting/register/_g4sKy2KTYGMIhtNIAxGeA#/registration Register to get link]. ** [[d:Wikidata:Thirteenth Birthday|Wikidata’s 13th Birthday]] is just around the corner! Are you planning to host a celebration but haven’t added it to the calendar yet? Join the party! Thirty-nine (39) events are already scheduled, and yours can be too: [[d:Wikidata:Thirteenth Birthday/Calendar|Birthday Calendar]]. It’s also the perfect time to contribute a birthday present or message to [[d:Wikidata:Thirteenth Birthday/Presents & messages|this list]]. Gifts can be anything that celebrates Wikidata and its amazing community like a script you’ve written, a tool improvement, a visual, a poem... 🎉 ''' Press, articles, blog posts, videos ''' * Videos: (French) [https://m.youtube.com/watch?v=RbxuPd7eprk Formation Wikidata 2 de WikiSkills] By Africa Wiki Women ''' Tool of the week ''' * [https://wikidata-todo.toolforge.org/duplicity/#/ Duplicity] This tool can pick a random article on a wiki without associated Wikidata item, and offer some possible matches on Wikidata, so you can add it to an existing item, or create a new one. == Wikifunctions & Abstract Wikipedia Newsletter #219 is out: Accessing qualifiers in Wikidata statements == There is [[:f:Special:MyLanguage/Wikifunctions:Status updates/2025-09-26|a new update]] for Wikifunctions and Abstract Wikipedia. Please, come and read it! In this issue, we introduce the possibility of calling also qualifiers from Wikidata, we update you on the recent deployments of Wikifunctions and on the Wikifunctions presentations at Wikimedia events, we give you the recent updates on Types, and we take a look at the latest software developments. Want to catch up with the previous updates? Check [[:f:Special:MyLanguage/Wikifunctions:Status updates|our archive]]! [[File:Wikidata-logo-en.svg|150px|right]]
'' Here's your quick overview of what has been happening around Wikidata in the
week leading up to 2025-09-29. Missed the previous one? See issue [[d:Special:MyLanguage/Wikidata:Status updates/Previous|#698]].
''
''' Discussions ''' * Open request for adminship: [[d:Wikidata:Requests_for_permissions/Administrator/Empat_Tilda|Empat Tilda]]: RfP scheduled to end after 3 October 2025 18:30 (UTC) ''' Events ''' * [[d:Special:MyLanguage/Wikidata:Events|Upcoming events]]: ** New Linked Data for Libraries [[d:Wikidata: WikiProject LD4 Wikidata Affinity Group|LD4 Wikidata Affinity Group]] project series! Mark your calendars for our first session of our WikiProject Personal Pronouns event series, which will provide a high-level overview of WikiProject Personal Pronouns' goals, past work, and implementation plans. The session will also serve as a practical orientation to the implementation work for this project, ending with a demonstration of the workflow to be used in subsequent sessions. No previous Wikidata experience is needed to participate. This session will be recorded. Slides and recordings will be found on the project page after the session. Join us Tuesday, September 30th at 9am PT/ 12pm ET/ 16:00 UTC / 6pm CEST ([https://www.timeanddate.com/worldclock/fixedtime.html?msg=LD4+Wikidata+Affinity+Group+Project+Series+Call&iso=20250930T09&p1=234&ah=1 Time zone converter]). Please see our [[d:Wikidata:WikiProject LD4 Wikidata Affinity Group/Project Series/PersonalPronouns|project page]] for more information and Zoom links. ** Wikidata are hosting a [[d:Event:Embedding_Project_Webinar|webinar on the Embedding Project]]: AI/ML Project Manager Philippe Saadé (WMDE) will introduce the project, including early experiences, wins and pitfalls, and open up the floor to your questions. *** Takes place 1600 - 1700 UTC, October 9, 2025. *** Embedding Project? [[d:Wikidata:Embedding_Project|Read about it here]] *** Want to join the Webinar? [[d:Special:EventDetails/2196|Please register here with your Wiki account]] ** 🎁 Countdown to 13!🎁 Wikidata's 13th Birthday is fast approaching and this week marks the start of the first Birthday Events! For a full rundown of all Birthday Events, please see the [[d:Wikidata:Thirteenth_Birthday/Calendar|Birthday Calendar]].
Starting the month of celebrations this week include: Wali Wikimedians Community, Latin America in Wikidata 2025, Hausa Wikipedians User Group and the language communities of Zambia, Twi and Kinyarwanda and regional celebrations from the Northern Nigerian, Dagbani and Ghanaians Wikimedian communities! ''' Press, articles, blog posts, videos ''' * Blogs: ** [https://tech-news.wikimedia.de/2025/09/19/enhanced-search-for-wikibase/ Enhanced Search for Wikibase] ** [https://cthoyt.com/2025/09/25/enriching-ror-with-wikidata.html Suggesting new relations in ROR from Wikidata] By Charles Tapley Hoyt * Papers: ** [https://arxiv.org/pdf/2505.15501 Protoknowledge Shapes Behaviour of LLMs: Text-to-SPARQL] ** [https://orb.binghamton.edu/librarian_fac/117/ Wikidata Workflows: with ORCiD and OpenRefine - a Binghamton University Case Study] By Sasha Frizzell - This lightning talk outlines Binghamton University Libraries’ pilot project to convert ORCID faculty data into Wikidata entries, showcasing linked data integration, data cleaning workflows, and strategies for enhancing institutional research visibility. * Videos: ** [https://m.youtube.com/watch?v=PJSWWGuPzJE Wikidata tutorial: how to merge duplicate items] ** [https://m.youtube.com/watch?v=DeHIq3wX7GE Wikidata Wikishootme SAT] ** [https://m.youtube.com/watch?v=gEVKAoQc0jM How to Fetch Subclasses and Parent Classes in Wikidata SPARQL Queries] ** (Portuguese) [https://m.youtube.com/watch?v=41FtK-tbrK4 Black women in history through Wikidata] ** [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=nkUjnDEZuYU Provenance and Restitution: Shared knowledge graphs as a tool in recovering looted cultural heritage] ''' Tool of the week ''' *[https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/User:Jon_Harald_S%C3%B8by/senseItemLabel.js senseItemLabel] is a UserScript by [[User:Jon Harald Søby]] The Script Lets you add the lemma for the current lexeme as a label to items added to the lexeme's senses as [https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Property:P5137 item for this sense (P5137)] in one click. ''' * Query examples: **[https://w.wiki/FRz9 maps of running and closed railway stations in Denmark] ** [https://w.wiki/FUDK Formula One Teams by employee count] - source: Discord * WikiProject Highlights: ** [[d:Wikidata:WikiProject_Piano_Rolls|Piano Rolls]] - has bee greatly expanded and subpages added. ** [[d:Wikidata:WikiProject_Open_Topstukken_Maastricht_University_and_Radboud_University/Manual|Open Topstukken Maastricht University and Radboud University]] - A user manual for contributing. * [[d:Wikidata:Showcase items|Showcase Items]]: [[d:Q1059888|Because You Left (Q1059888)]] - episode of Lost (S5 E1) * [[d:Wikidata:Showcase lexemes|Showcase Lexemes]]:[[d:Lexeme:L298686|Knoten (L298686)]] - German noun (ˈknoːtn̩) meaning "tied fastening of ropes/strings", "speed unit in aviation/shipping", or "tangled clump of hair" ''' Development ''' * Mobile statement editing: We continue to make good progress, which you can check out on [https://www.wikidata.beta.wmcloud.org/w/index.php?title=Q593112&mobileaction=toggle_view_mobile beta Wikidata]. Specifically it is now possible to ** save changes to edits made to string Properties on statements (without losing qualifier / reference information) ([[phab:T401405]]) ** change the rank of statements ([[phab:T402436]]) * Query Service UI: jhsoby submitted patches for two issues related to the example dialog. Thank you! ([[phab:T405747]], [[phab:T405720]]) * Lexicographical data: 1F616EMO submitted a patch to fix a localization issue on the Lexeme page. Thank you! ([[phab:T322526]]) * Dumps: We fixed issues with the n-triples and truthy dumps generation and set up a [https://grafana.wikimedia.org/d/6c666ea5-01de-4325-9c32-41ee09882368/wikidata-data-dumps-monitoring?orgId=1&from=now-90d&to=now&timezone=utc dashboard for monitoring] ([[phab:T403882]]) * GraphQL: We are continuing to make labels of linked entities accessible in the GraphQL endpoint ([[phab:T404692]]) * Wikidata in Wikipedia and co: We are continuing to work on improvements to how we technically track the use of Wikidata in the other Wikimedia projects in order to reduce the amount of changes from Wikidata in the watchlist and recent changes on Wikipedia and co. * Wikidata Query Service GUI: We deployed two fixes by [[:d:User:Jon Harald Søby|User:Jon Harald Søby]], thank you! ([[phab:T405720]], [[phab:T405747]]) [[phab:maniphest/query/4RotIcw5oINo/#R|You can see all open tickets related to Wikidata here]]. If you want to help, you can also have a look at [https://phabricator.wikimedia.org/project/board/71/query/zfiRgTnZF7zu/?filter=zfiRgTnZF7zu&order=priority the tasks needing a volunteer]. ''' Weekly Tasks ''' * Add labels, in your own language(s), for the new properties listed [[d:Wikidata:Status_updates/Next#Newest_properties_and_property_proposals_to_review|above]]. * Contribute to the showcase Item and Lexeme [[d:Wikidata:Status_updates/Next#Did_you_know?|above]]. * Govdirectory weekly focus country: [[d:Wikidata:WikiProject Govdirectory/Bulgaria|Bulgaria]] * Summarize your [[d:Wikidata:Status_updates/Next#Did_you_know?|WikiProject's ongoing activities]] in one or two sentences. * Help [[d:Special:LanguageStats|translate]] or proofread the interface and documentation pages, in your own language! * [[d:User:Pasleim/projectmerge|Help merge identical items]] across Wikimedia projects. * Help [[d:Wikidata:Status updates/Next|write the next summary!]]
